danger_signals.visual_form: High density of enemy sprites on screen during combat sequences
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent threat; player must be alert and react quickly to avoid damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Predominantly dark-themed stages and blackened landscapes
danger_signals.meaning: Dangerous territory; the game world exists in shadow, reflecting the hidden nature of ninja clans and their deadly conflict

danger_signals.visual_form: Recurring enemy sprite patterns throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Persistent threat from faction forces; danger is constant and varied only in positioning, not in enemy type

danger_signals.visual_form: Hagane's mechanical/cybernetic appearance contrasted with human enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Hagane is a survivor of near-death transformation; visual manifestation of vulnerability turned into weaponized danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items that increase the player's health meter beyond the base three hits
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary reprieve from danger; resources to survive longer in hostile environments

safety_signals.visual_form: Completion of platforming sections and combat arenas
safety_signals.meaning: Advancement toward the objective; moving through the Koma clan's territory toward revenge fulfillment

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Fuma clan members distinguished by zodiac affiliation (visual variants not detailed in source)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Living mainly in darkness; protected territories housing the Holy Grail; bloodlines isolated from outside world

faction_indicators.color_identity: White, Gold, and Red Dragon factions
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dragon-based color coding system for clan divisions
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Three distinct color-coded dragon factions (white, gold, red) allow visual differentiation of enemy types and organizational structure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Evil clan territory; complex structures that can be destroyed; represent the destructive force threatening the world

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Holy Grail protected by Fuma clan in hidden darkness
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fuma clan maintains isolation and spiritual duty; Koma clan plots destruction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark stages filled with enemies; blackened landscapes; Koma clan complex structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively patrol and attack; hostile forces occupy formerly Fuma-protected territory; Hagane must fight through multiple faction forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Koma clan complex destroyed; blackened land overlooked from cliff; Hagane's glowing cyborg eyes fade to black; nature reclaims the corrupted landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hagane ceases movement (passes away); time and rust claim his form; the world begins healing as the threat dissipates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cyborg body replacing human form; mechanical upgrades visible in character design; contrast between human brain and technological body
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Superhuman speed and strength; capability to single-handedly destroy an entire evil clan; powered by vengeance

environmental_storytelling.example: Koma clan's 'complex' structures dominate stages, communicating their occupation of previously sacred Fuma territory
Koma clan's three dragon factions (white, gold, red) are visually distinguished, allowing players to understand clan hierarchy through enemy appearance
Both ninja clans 'live mainly in darkness,' which manifests in the game's predominantly dark-themed visual palette, reinforcing the hidden, shadowy nature of their conflict
The ending shows 'blackened land' at the Koma complex, with nature reclaiming it after the clan's destruction—visual progression of world healing after threat elimination
Hagane's mechanical body stands out among human enemies, visually communicating his status as neither fully human nor fully machine—a survivor transformed into a weapon

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Executes somersaults, flying jump kicks, wall-jumping, sliding, and charge attacks; switches between four weapons (sword, chain, shuriken, grenades); uses magic attacks to clear screen
Settles on cliff overlooking destroyed Koma complex; glowing cyborg eyes fade to black; body rusts as time passes; ceases all movement
Patrol stages; initiate combat with player; appear in high abundance to create persistent threat pressure
One survivor (Hagane) escaped death; clan maintains isolation and duty; members know nothing of other faction details except existence

notes: Hagane communicates world-state primarily through environmental darkness (danger/hidden conflict), enemy abundance (persistent threat), and faction color-coding (organizational structure). The ending is particularly rich in visual storytelling: the transition from dark blackened landscape to nature's reclamation signals moral and worldly healing after the destructive Koma clan is eliminated. Hagane's cyborg nature—visible in his mechanical appearance and glowing eyes—is a visual manifestation of resurrection-through-technology and superhuman revenge capability. The short stages with few checkpoints reinforce the desperate, relentless nature of the conflict. The game blends traditional Japanese ninja/samurai aesthetics with futuristic cybernetic elements, creating visual cognitive dissonance that mirrors Hagane's own hybrid nature.
